title: श्री सरस्वती स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सरस्वती स्तुति ॥

सरस्वति नमस्तुभ्यं वरदे कामरूपिणि।
विद्यारम्भं करिष्यामि सिद्धिर्भवतु मे सदा॥१॥

पद्मपत्रविशालाक्षि पद्मकेसरवर्णिनि।
नित्यं पद्मालया देवि सा मां पातु सरस्वती॥२॥

या वेदान्तार्थतत्त्वज्ञा या कृत्स्नं वेदमार्गदा।
यार्थानामपि या चार्था ताम्अहं शरणं गतः॥३॥

शरत्कालजलधारा सूक्ष्मधारा शुचिस्मिता।
वाग्देवता महाभागा सा मां पातु सरस्वती॥४॥

ममोपजातं सकलं विनाशय सरस्वति।
तमो ग्रहजनाद्दोषं बुद्धिसंयुतया गिरा॥५॥

वीणापुस्तकधारिण्यै विद्याधिष्ठातृदेवतायै।
सर्वसिद्धिकरी देवी सदा तुष्टा सरस्वती॥६॥

सुरासुरैश्च सम्पूज्या ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवात्मिका।
वेदशास्त्रार्थदात्री या सरस्वत्यै नमोऽस्तु ते॥७॥

वागर्थे विस्मृते यत्र स्मृतिं तत्र प्रयच्छ मे।
त्वदीयकृपया देवि सर्वसिद्धिं प्रयच्छ मे॥८॥

चतुर्भुजे चन्द्रवर्णे पद्मासनसुसंस्थिते।
मम वाक्सिद्धिमाधत्स्व अजस्रं हि सरस्वती॥९॥

पावनी पापनाशिनी धर्मार्थकाममोक्षदा।
ज्ञानदानमयी देवी सदा मे शुभमाप्नुहि॥१०॥

नमोऽस्तु सरस्वत्यै देव्यै शरद्जोत्स्नासमप्रभायै।
वाञ्छितं मे वरं देहि कीर्तिं विद्यां सुखं बलम्॥११॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
सरस्वतीस्तुतिं चैतां नित्यं भक्त्या पठेन्नरः।
विद्या कीर्तिः सुखं तस्य लभते नात्र संशयः॥

॥ इति श्री सरस्वती स्तुतिः सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Sarasvatī Stuti ||

Sarasvati namastubhyaṁ varade kāmarūpiṇi.
Vidyārambhaṁ kariṣyāmi siddhirbhavatu me sadā.||1||

Padmapatraviśālākṣi padmakesaravarṇini.
Nityaṁ padmālayā devī sā māṁ pātu Sarasvatī.||2||

Yā vedāntārthattattvajñā yā kṛtsnaṁ vedamārgadā.
Yārthānāmapi yā chārthā tāmahaṁ śaraṇaṁ gataḥ.||3||

Śaratkālajaladharā sūkṣmadhārā śuchismitā.
Vāgdevatā Mahābhāgā sā māṁ pātu Sarasvatī.||4||

Mamopajātaṁ sakalaṁ vināśaya Sarasvati.
Tamo grahājanāddoṣaṁ buddhisaṁyutayā girā.||5||

Vīṇāpustakadhāriṇyai vidyādhiṣṭhātṛdevatāyai.
Sarvasiddhikarī devī sadā tuṣṭā Sarasvatī.||6||

Surāsuraiścha sampūjyā Brahmaviṣṇuśivātmikā.
Vedaśāstrārthadātrī yā Sarasvatyai namo'stu te.||7||

Vāgārthe vismṛte yatra smṛtiṁ tatra prayachchha me.
Tvadīyakṛpayā devi sarvasiddhiṁ prayachchha me.||8||

Chaturbhuje chandravarṇe padmāsanasusaṁsthite.
Mama vāksiddhimādhatsva ajasraṁ hi Sarasvatī.||9||

Pāvanī pāpanāśinī dharmārthakāmamokṣadā.
Jñānadānamayī devī sadā me śubhamāpnuhi.||10||

Namo'stu Sarasvatyai devyai śaradjotsnāsamaprabhāyai.
Vāñchhitaṁ me varaṁ dehi kīrtiṁ vidyāṁ sukhaṁ balam.||11||

|| Phalaśruti ||
Sarasvatīstutiṁ chaitāṁ nityaṁ bhaktyā paṭhennaraḥ.
Vidyā kīrtiḥ sukhaṁ tasya labhate nātra saṁśayaḥ.

|| Iti Shrī Sarasvatī Stutiḥ sampūrṇā ||
hindiMeaning: (1) हे सरस्वती! वरदायिनी! कामरूपिणी! आपको नमस्कार। मैं विद्या का आरम्भ करता हूँ, मुझे सदा सिद्धि प्राप्त हो। (2) पद्मपत्र जैसे विशाल नेत्रों वाली, कमल के केसर जैसी वर्णवाली, नित्य कमलों में निवास करने वाली सरस्वती देवी मेरी रक्षा करें। (3) जो वेदान्त के अर्थ-तत्त्व को जानने वाली हैं, समस्त वेदमार्ग देने वाली हैं, जो अर्थों का भी अर्थ हैं, उनकी शरण में जाता हूँ। (4) शरत्काल की जलधारा जैसी, सूक्ष्मधारा, शुचि मुस्कान वाली, महाभाग वाग्देवता सरस्वती मेरी रक्षा करें। (5) हे सरस्वती! मेरे उत्पन्न हुए तम, ग्रह दोष सबको बुद्धियुक्त वाणी से नष्ट करो। (6-11) वीणा-पुस्तक धारिणी, विद्या की अधिष्ठात्री, देवासुरों द्वारा पूज्य, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शिव स्वरूपिणी, वाक्सिद्धि देने वाली, पापनाशिनी, धर्म-अर्थ-काम-मोक्ष प्रदा देवी से कीर्ति, विद्या, सुख और बल की प्रार्थना।
benefits: Invokes knowledge, fame, happiness, and strength without doubt
Traditionally recited for removal of darkness, planetary afflictions, and mental dullness
Invokes the blessing of mastery of speech and eloquence (vak siddhi)
Purifies sins and grants all four Purusharthas
Ideal for recitation before beginning any study or education
Simple yet powerful stuti suitable for daily recitation
description: Saraswati Stuti — श्री सरस्वती स्तुति — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Saraswati. Rooted in Puranic Compilation, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to purify sins and grant all four Purusharthas as well as remove darkness, planetary afflictions, and mental dullness. Additionally, it is known to grant knowledge, fame, happiness, and strength without doubt. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vasant Panchami for enhanced blessings. With 11 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Saraswati Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Saraswati Stuti in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer to Goddess Saraswati for knowledge, speech mastery, fame, and success.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Saraswati Stuti and what does it pray for?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Stuti (श्री सरस्वती स्तुति) is a beloved 11-verse Sanskrit hymn of praise (stuti) addressed to Devi Sarasvati — the goddess of knowledge (vidya), speech (vak), music, the arts and the four Vedas. The stuti opens with the famous line 'Sarasvati namastubhyam varade kamarupini, vidyarambham karishyami siddhirbhavatu me sada' — 'Salutations to you Saraswati, granter of boons, who takes any form desired; I am beginning my study, may I always attain success.' This single sloka is recited universally before any learning, examination, study session or auspicious commencement of education across India. The full stuti continues to praise her as Vagdevata (deity of speech), Vinapustakadharini (holder of vina and book) and Brahmavishnushivatmika (one with the trinity), preserved in classical Puranic compilation tradition. (Sources: Puranic Sarasvati stotra tradition; Sarasvati Rahasya Upanishad lineage.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Sarasvati namastubhyam varade kamarupini?
seoFaqs.answer: This opening verse — Sarasvati namastubhyam varade kamarupini, vidyarambham karishyami siddhirbhavatu me sada — is among the most recited slokas in the entire Hindu tradition. Word-by-word: Sarasvati — the goddess of speech, knowledge and the flowing river of wisdom; namah tubhyam — salutations to you; varade — O granter of boons; kamarupini — O one who takes any form at will (the unrestricted manifestation of pure consciousness); vidyarambham karishyami — I am about to begin (my) study/learning; siddhi bhavatu me sada — may success/fulfilment always be mine. Children across India recite this single verse before opening a textbook, students before an examination, musicians before a recital, scholars before composing a work, and devotees before commencing any auspicious learning. (Sources: classical Sarasvati Stuti tradition; school and gurukula recitation paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Saraswati Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Stuti is most powerful when recited on Vasant Panchami — the great festival of Saraswati on Magha Shukla Panchami (typically late January or February), when she is worshipped with yellow flowers, yellow sweets, and the formal Akshara-abhyasa (first-letter writing) ceremony for children. It is also especially auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar — the day of the guru and learning), early morning at Brahma muhurta before commencing study, before any examination, before opening a new book, before beginning a new course of study, on Saraswati Avahana and Saraswati Visarjana days during Navaratri (Maha Saptami to Vijayadashami) when scholarly tools are formally worshipped, and on Vijayadashami itself which is the traditional day for Akshara-abhyasa and Vidyarambha. (Sources: Drik Panchang Sarasvati festival calendar; classical Sarasvati upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do students gain from reciting the Saraswati Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional teachers attribute the following benefits to regular recitation of the Saraswati Stuti by students and seekers of knowledge: removal of mental dullness (jadya-nashana) and improvement in memory (smriti-vriddhi), grace of Devi for vak-siddhi (mastery and clarity of speech), success in examinations, interviews and competitive tests, removal of planetary afflictions (graha-dosha) that obstruct learning especially weak Mercury (Budha) and weak Jupiter (Guru), clarity of intellect (buddhi-shuddhi) for grasping difficult subjects, fluency in expression for writers, speakers, teachers and musicians, the four purusharthas — dharma, artha, kama and moksha — and the supreme phala promised in the phalashruti, which is vidya, kirti (fame) and sukha (happiness) without doubt. (Sources: Saraswati Stuti phalashruti; classical guru-shishya teaching tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How should the Saraswati Stuti be recited for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Sarasvati upasana paddhati prescribes the following vidhi: (1) Bathe in the early morning, wear clean white or yellow clothes (the colours of Sarasvati); (2) Sit on a clean white asana facing east or northeast; (3) Place a picture or murti of Devi Sarasvati on a chowki with a white cloth; (4) Light a ghee lamp and incense; (5) Offer white flowers (especially jasmine, lotus or champa), akshat, sandalwood paste, and a naivedya of white sweets, kheer, or sugar-rice; (6) Meditate on Devi as four-armed, white-complexioned, holding vina, book, mala and varada-mudra, seated on a white lotus or swan; (7) Recite the stuti with full understanding, ideally three or eleven times; (8) Conclude with the phalashruti and Saraswati Vandana mantra Yaa Kundendu Tushaarahaaradhavalaa. (Sources: classical Sarasvati puja paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Saraswati Stuti the same as the Saraswati Vandana Yaa Kundendu Tushaarahaaradhavalaa?
seoFaqs.answer: No, the two are distinct compositions, though both are short Sanskrit prayers addressed to Devi Sarasvati. The Saraswati Stuti is an 11-verse Puranic-tradition hymn opening with Sarasvati namastubhyam varade kamarupini, used as a preliminary prayer before study. The Saraswati Vandana — Yaa Kundendu Tushaarahaaradhavalaa Yaa Shubhravastraavritaa — is a single, exquisitely-crafted dhyana sloka that is the most famous of all Sarasvati prayers in modern India, recited daily in schools across the country at morning assembly. The Vandana describes Devi as white as kunda flower, moon, snow and pearl-strand, clad in white garments, holding the most beautiful vina, seated on a white lotus, ever worshipped by Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva, beseeching her to remove all jadya (dullness). They are often recited together. (Sources: classical Sarasvati stotra anthologies; school morning prayer tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Which form of Saraswati is described in the Saraswati Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Stuti describes Devi in her classical iconographic and theological forms across its eleven verses. Iconographically she is Padmapatra-vishalakshi (with eyes wide as lotus petals), Padmakesara-varnini (with the colour of lotus filaments), Nityam-padmalaya (eternally seated on lotus), Vinapustakadharini (holder of vina and book), Chaturbhuje (four-armed), Chandravarne (moon-complexioned), Padmasana-susamsthite (seated firmly on a lotus seat), and Sharad-jyotsna-samaprabhayai (radiant as autumnal moonlight). Theologically she is Vedanta-artha-tattva-jna (knower of the deepest truths of Vedanta), Vagdevata (the very deity of speech), Vidya-adhisthatri-devata (presiding deity of all knowledge), Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva-atmika (one with the trimurti), and Dharma-artha-kama-moksha-prada (granter of all four purusharthas). (Sources: Saraswati Stuti verses 2, 6, 9; classical Sarasvati iconography.)
